tool_args.label: Report to the Finance Committee 2025 Year End Review

payload.title: Report to the Finance Committee 2025 Year End Review
payload.description: MTA Finance Department 2025 Year End Review report to the Finance Committee covering capital program funding, credit ratings, market recap, 2026 financing calendar, and debt overview

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

payload.description: MTA Finance Department, prepared the 2025 Year End Review report to the Finance Committee

warnings: entity.office is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Office names are relation context unless the source treats the office as the entity.

tool_args.label: Olga Chernat, Deputy Chief, Financial Services

warnings: entity.organization is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Organization names should link to entity records when supported. Suggested relation: part_of_agency.

payload.raw_value_text: $1.6B issued in January 2025
approximately $800M capacity expected in 2026 or later
$15 billion of transit and commuter projects in the 2020-2024 Capital Program

tool_args.label: Bonds Notes Loans for Capital Projects

payload.raw_value_text: $4.3 billion of bonds, notes, and loans to finance capital projects
$146 million saved in debt service over the financial plan period by refinancing $2.7 billion of outstanding bonds
refinancing $2.7 billion of outstanding bonds
$1.1 billion in long-term debt paid off at maturity
Issuance reached a record $579.9 billion (+12.9% YoY)
municipal bond funds saw strong inflows ($26.6 billion net)
Municipal issuance projected at about $600 billion
Total New Money Issuance $5,650 million
Debt repayment of $2.1 billion will offset new money issuance
$3.1 billion of MTA & TBTA bonds eligible to be refunded for savings (market-dependent)
As of 12/31/2025, $48.1 billion debt outstanding
2024 -> 2025: Net increase of $2.9 billion (+6.4%)

tool_args.label: Capital Lockbox Fund Debt Growth Component

payload.raw_value_text: $2 billion of the net increase due to growth in Capital Lockbox Fund debt
$917 million increase in Operating Budget debt
Projected 2026: Debt Outstanding is projected to increase by $3.6 billion

tool_args.label: Hudson Rail Yards Trust Obligations Excluded

payload.raw_value_text: Excludes $740.98 million Hudson Rail Yards Trust Obligations (Series 2016A and Series 2020A)

payload.description: $1.6B RETT Bonds issued to fund 2020-2024 and future transit and commuter projects
Transportation Revenue Bonds (TRB) Ratings Upgrade - Moody's: A3 → A2
Transportation Revenue Bonds (TRB) Ratings Upgrade - S&P: A- → A
Report to the Finance Committee 2025 Year End Review published
